sentence: וַיֹּאמֶר אֱלֹהִים יְהִי מְאֹרֹת בִּרְקִיעַ הַשָּׁמַיִם לְהַבְדִּיל בֵּין הַיּוֹם וּבֵין הַלָּיְלָה וְהָיוּ לְאֹתֹת וּלְמוֹעֲדִים וּלְיָמִים וְשָׁנִים

grammatical-notes.בִּרְקִיעַ: Preposition + Construct. 'In the hammered-out expanse of'.
grammatical-notes.וּלְמוֹעֲדִים: Conjunction + Preposition + Noun 'moed'. Appointed times or seasonal festivals.

dictionary-meaning.מְאֹרֹת: Light-bearing regulators — disclosing the instrumental prefix 'me-' showing these are tools for light management (Rule 21).
dictionary-meaning.לְאֹתֹת: For specific signs — markers for direction and communication.
dictionary-meaning.וּלְמוֹעֲדִים: For appointed times — disclosing the 'fixed times' of seasons and calendars (Rule 21).

main-translation: ***Then Elohim decreed***, '***Let existence be granted to light-bearing regulators within the hammered-out expanse of the skies to establish a sovereign and fixed boundary between the day and the night; and let them serve as markers for specific signs, for appointed times, and for days and years.***'

main-translation-explanation.light-bearing regulators: INTERNAL ADVERSARIAL TRIAL: Challenged 'lights'. REFINEMENT: Chose 'light-bearing regulators' to disclose the instrumental nature of meorot (light-holders) and their management function (Rule 21).
main-translation-explanation.markers for specific signs, for appointe: INTERNAL ADVERSARIAL TRIAL: Disclosed the 'moed' as fixed times rather than just vague 'seasons' (Rule 21).

rejected-translations.reason: Thin translation; fails to disclose the 'instrumental' nature of the meorot.
